title: Springy Sour Kenyan Swede Stew
description: A sour and springy Kenyan dish featuring swede, this stew is perfect for a cold evening, with a unique spherified twist and low glycemic index.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the diced swede and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the lamb broth, sour cream, lemon juice, and chili flakes, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix the sodium alginate with the water to create a spherification bath.
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the stew into the spherification bath to create spherified bites.
Remove the spherified bites from the bath with a slotted spoon and add to the stew.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
